healthcareTargatedDrawHeading: Category-Based Express Entry Selection Draws: Healthcare Draw
healthcareTargatedDrawPara: In 2023, the Canadian Government launched the Express Entry healthcare draws, a strategic initiative aimed at attracting and retaining crucial healthcare professionals like nurses, physicians, and dentists. This program fast-tracks immigration applications, significantly reducing the time between application and approval for permanent residency. The process, managed by Canada's Immigration, Refugees, and Citizenship Canada (IRCC), aims to process the majority of applications within six months. However, it's important to note that this is not a guaranteed timeline. The urgent need for additional healthcare workers in Canada is evident, and the Express Entry healthcare draws are a crucial tool in addressing this issue.
EligibilityCriteriaHeading: Eligibility Criteria for Express Entry Healthcare Draws
EligPara1: Understanding the process of becoming eligible for Express Entry healthcare draws requires a comprehensive understanding of the Express Entry system itself.
EligPara2: Express Entry is not an immigration program but a system used to manage several Canadian immigration programs. It operates on an expression of interest intake model, where you submit your profile into a pool of candidates and wait for an invitation to apply for permanent residency.
EligPara3: To be eligible for Express Entry, you must meet the criteria for one of the following programs:
EligPara4: Once you've submitted your profile, your eligibility for each program is evaluated based on your work experience, education, age, and language proficiency. This evaluation is conducted using the Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS), which assigns a score to your profile.
EligPara5: Your CRS score is crucial as it determines whether you receive an invitation to apply (ITA) in a round of invitations, also known as a draw. In an all-program draw, the highest-scoring candidates are invited, while in a category-based draw, the top-scoring candidates who meet the draw's category eligibility criteria are invited.
EligPara6: Healthcare Express Entry draws are category-based draws. To be eligible for the health worker category, you must have at least six months of full-time work experience in an eligible healthcare occupation within the past three years.
EligibleOccupationsHeading: Eligible Occupations for Express Entry Healthcare Draws
EligibleOccupationsPara: In order to be eligible for Express Entry Healthcare draws, you must have at least six months of full-time (or the equivalent in part-time) paid work experience within the three years preceding the issuance of an ITA and when IRCC receives your application for Canadian permanent residency.
eo1: Within the last 3 years, you have at least 6 months of full-time, continuous work experience (or an equivalent amount of part-time work experience) in a single eligible National Occupational Classification (NOC) in the healthcare sector.
EligibleOccupTableHeading: **The following table contains the most up-to-date list of eligible occupations. Remember that Canada may change these occupations in response to labor market conditions.
eoT1Li8: Kinesiologists and other professional occupations in therapy and assessment
eoT1Li11: Medical laboratory assistants and related technical occupations
eoT1Li15: Nurse aides, orderlies, and patient service associates
eoT1Li20: Other assisting occupations in support of health services
eoT1Li22: Other professional occupations in health diagnosing and treating
eoT1Li23: Other technical occupations in therapy and assessment
eoT1Li25: Pharmacy technical assistants and pharmacy assistants
eoT1Li26: Physician assistants, midwives, and allied health professionals
eoT1Li29: Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses
eoT1Li30: Respiratory therapists, clinical perfusionists, and cardiopulmonary technologists
eoT1Li31: Specialists in clinical and laboratory medicine
eoT1Li33: Therapists in counselling and related specialized therapies
eoT1Li34: Traditional Chinese medicine practitioners and acupuncturists
hehc1: The first-ever Express Entry draw for targeted healthcare occupation workers was held in February 2024. Express Entry draw #284 saw a CRS score of 422 and invited 3500 candidates eligible for category-based selection in healthcare occupations.
HowApplyHeading: How to Apply for Express Entry Healthcare Draws
ha1: Submit your profile to the Express Entry pool
ha2: Prove that you have at least six months of eligible work experience in healthcare (within the three years preceding the issuance of an ITA and receipt of your application by IRCC).
ha3: Await an ITA (or take steps to improve your CRS score to increase your chances of receiving an ITA)
ha4: Respond to the ITA by submitting your application for permanent residency
ha5: Wait for a decision from an IRCC officer. If approved, you can become a permanent resident of Canada.
haRemb: Remember, the key to success in the Express Entry system is to ensure you meet all the eligibility requirements and continuously work on improving your CRS score.

q1: Who is eligible for healthcare Express Entry?
qa1: To be eligible for a healthcare occupations category Express Entry draw, candidates are required to: Have accumulated, within the past three years, continuous work experience* that equates to a minimum of six months of full-time (or an equal amount of part-time work experience). Check our eligibility section for more details.
q2: What is the lowest CRS score for healthcare workers?
qa2: A On February 14, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC) conducted a category-based draw for Express Entry candidates with experience in healthcare occupations where the departmentissued 3,500 Invitations to Apply (ITAs) to those with a minimum Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) score of 422.
q3: Can I move to Canada as a health care assistant?
qa3: To move to Canada as a healthcare worker, applicants must obtain accreditation or licensing to work in a Canadian province. Note: Since the licensing & accreditation process of healthcare professionals varies by region, each province has its own institution that licenses Internationally Educated Nurses (IEN).
q4: What is the Express Entry program for health workers in Canada?
qa4: Canada desperately needs additional healthcare workers to work in Canada. Express Entry healthcare draws were introduced in 2023 as one of the levers at the disposal of the Government of Canada to attract and retain these badly needed nurses, physicians, dentists, and other healthcare workers. (see above table to see which NOC qualify under healthcare category)
q5: Can nurses apply for Express Entry in Canada?
qa5:
As a nurse, you may be qualified to come to Canada as a skilled worker through one of the many economic immigration programs at both the federal and provincial level.

There are four main ways that you can come to Canada as a nurse:

  • Express Entry (Healthcare Targeted Draws)
  • Quebec Immigration
  • Provincial Nominee Programs
  • Study in Canada
q6: What is the age limit for nursing in Canada?
qa6: There are no specific age restrictions for pursuing a nursing career in Canada as an Indian. Your eligibility will depend on meeting nursing regulatory bodies' educational and language requirements. As long as you meet these requirements, you can pursue a nursing career regardless of age.
q7: If I have a targeted Healthcare Occupation, does my CRS score still matter?
qa7: Your CRS score remains crucial even with a targeted Healthcare occupation. However, in Healthcare - specific draws, the CRS score needed to secure an ITA tends to be lower compared to comprehensive program draws. For example, in the inaugural Trade-specific draw, the CRS cutoff stood at 422, notably lower than the 524 required in concurrent all-program draws.
q8: How often IRCC will hold Healthcare -specific draws?
qa8: There is no set schedule of Healthcare -specific draws by IRCC. However, IRCC has indicated a commitment to prioritize Healthcare -specific draws in the future, although no specific timeline has been announced.
q9: Do I need to provide extra documents if I am selected in a Healthcare - Specific draw?
qa9: Additional documents are not required; however, it's essential to ensure that the provided work experience documents distinctly display a minimum of 6 months of Healthcare experience within the past 3 years.
q10: My spouse qualifies for a Healthcare -specific draw, can my profile receive the ITA?
qa10: In a Healthcare -specific draw, the principal applicant must possess the required experience to receive an ITA. If your spouse meets the criteria, they should create their own Express Entry profile where they are listed as the principal applicant.
